retrieved_docs.content: A large cohort study involving 774 patients in the United States found that the ExoDx assay, in conjunction with standards of care features, outperformed the ExoDx assay or standards of care variables when used alone in predicting the presence of Gleason grade 7 or above and negative biopsies. [96] Several exosomal metabolites, including survivin and claudin 3, have also been higher in the plasma of patients with prostate cancer. [98] [99] [100] ExoDx is also one of the tests recommended by the NCCN for prostate cancer risk stratification bioassay testing.

retrieved_docs.title: Biomarker Assays for Elevated Prostate-Specific Antigen Risk Analysis -- Diagnostic Tests -- Blood-Based Biomarkers
retrieved_docs.content: Even though PSA is an excellent and sensitive biomarker, the biomarker is organ-specific but not disease-specific. Prostatic basal layer and basement membrane violations allow PSA to escape prostate cells and enter the bloodstream, leading to an increase in serum PSA levels. [24] [25] This increase in serum PSA levels may occur in several prostatic pathologies, such as benign prostatic hyperplasia, prostatitis, or prostate malignancy, or transiently with prostatic manipulation, as in prostate biopsies, prostatic massage, or urethral catheterization. [23] [26] [27] [28] [29]

retrieved_docs.title: Prostate Cancer Tissue-Based Biomarkers -- Diagnostic Tests -- Tissue-Based Biomarkers
retrieved_docs.content: Technically, the Decipher test is a transcriptomic assay that uses the microarray method to study the expression of 22 genes, namely, LASP1, IQGAP3, NFIB, S1PR4, THBS2, ANO7, PCDH7, MYBPC1, EPPK1, TSBP, PBX1, NUSAP1, ZWILCH, UBE2C, CAMK2N1, RABGAP1, PCAT-32, GLYATL1P4, PCAT-80, and TNFRSF19. These genes play an important role in the cell cycle, cell growth, differentiation, and androgen receptor signaling. The assay was developed by comparing 192 RP samples from men with metastatic prostate cancer and 353 healthy controls. The initial validation produced an area-under-curve (AUC) of 0.90. [41] The Decipher biopsy test's score to predict metastasis has been validated with an AUC of 0.8. [42]

[96] The test is far more patient-friendly compared to similar bioassays as it does not require a digital rectal exam or prostatic massage, unlike previous urine-based tests for prostate cancer. The kit is designed for home use and can be mailed directly to the diagnostic laboratory by the patient, making it particularly useful for patients without a rectum for digital prostatic massage or those for whom the procedure is not feasible.

retrieved_docs.title: Ewing Sarcoma -- Histopathology
retrieved_docs.content: More than 80% of the cases exhibit high CD99 expression. This highly sensitive immunohistochemical biomarker likely plays a key role in facilitating the continued migration of leukocytes to endothelium; however, this biomarker lacks specificity, as it may also be detected in other sarcomas and lymphomas. [2] In addition to MIC2 gene product CD99, Ewing cells often express CD45, synaptophysin, chromogranin, vimentin, keratin, desmin, neuron-specific enolase, and S-100. However, this immunohistochemistry panel has also been limited by a need for more specificity. [2] Molecular genetic studies using fluorescence in situ hybridization and reverse transcription-polymerase chain reaction are required to make the definitive distinction.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 3 -- Community 1610
retrieved_docs.content: Diagnostic agents for bile duct patency testing and gastric secretion test diagnostic agents are both classified as “other diagnostic agents in ATC.” These agents are used for specialized diagnostic procedures: the former assess the openness of the bile ducts, while the latter evaluate gastric secretory function. By being grouped under the broader ATC category of other diagnostic agents, they share a common regulatory and therapeutic classification despite serving distinct clinical testing purposes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2660
retrieved_docs.content: Diagnostic procedures on heart and pericardium are classified as a subset of other diagnostic cardiovascular procedures, indicating that they belong to the broader group of cardiovascular diagnostic tests. Within this category, there is a further subdivision called Other diagnostic procedures on heart and pericardium, which represents a more specific group of tests focused on the heart and its surrounding pericardial sac. This hierarchical organization clarifies how heart‑ and pericardium‑specific diagnostics fit into the overall framework of cardiovascular diagnostic testing, aiding clinicians in selecting and categorizing appropriate investigative procedures for cardiac and pericardial conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 1 -- Community 198
retrieved_docs.content: The CDISC SDTM, SEND and broader Clinical Data Interchange Standards Consortium (CDISC) terminologies constitute a unified, multilingual framework that standardizes laboratory tests, chemical entities, anatomical structures, clinical procedures, disease classifications and social‑behavioral variables for consistent data capture in clinical and pre‑clinical research. Key laboratory concepts include Bone Mineral Test, selenium (السيلنيوم ++Se) and estrogen (الإستروجين); both selenium and estrogen are linked by an inverse‑isa relationship to the generic class “unspecified chemistry” (كيمياء غير محدد). Additional laboratory measurements are red blood cell count (عدد خلايا الدم الحمراء) and other routine chemistry analytes. retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 3 -- Community 1725
retrieved_docs.content: Inspection (procedure) is a diagnostic category that incorporates several specific methods for visual and functional assessment of internal organs. Among its techniques are mediastinoscopy and thoracoscopy performed with ventilation of the lung, which allow direct visualization and sampling of mediastinal and pleural structures while maintaining adequate respiratory support. Another method included under inspection is the combination of paracentesis and endoscopic examination of the ileum, enabling fluid analysis together with direct endoscopic evaluation of the distal small intestine. In addition, inspection procedures serve as the method of choice for other bowel diagnostic procedures, encompassing a range of endoscopic and minimally invasive approaches used to assess gastrointestinal pathology. Together, these methods provide clinicians with essential information for accurate diagnosis, treatment planning, and optimal patient care.

Serous choroidal detachment (تَنَكُّسُ المَشيمِيَّة) and metastatic disseminated retinitis AND retinochoroiditis are also included. retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 2 -- Community 993
retrieved_docs.content: The CDISC SDTM Terminology and the broader Clinical Data Interchange Standards Consortium (CDISC) Terminology constitute standardized vocabularies that capture a wide spectrum of clinical concepts, procedures, and laboratory measurements for use in clinical trials and patient‑care data exchange. Both terminologies include the Bone Mineral Density Test, indicating its recognition as a diagnostic assessment within the CDISC framework. Procedural concepts shared across the vocabularies comprise respiratory intubation, mechanical ventilation, thoracoscopic wedge resection of the lung, exploratory laparotomy, colonoscopy (“تنظير القولون”), and additional items such as “sexuální partneři,” “test cesty,” and “تقويم نظم القلب.” The CDISC SDTM Procedure Terminology explicitly lists respiratory intubation, mechanical ventilation, thoracoscopic wedge resection of the lung, and colonoscopy, while the CDISC SDTM Terminology further incorporates intrakavitární radioterapie (intracavitary radiotherapy), the anatomical structure larynx, and the laboratory measure “عدد خلايا الدم الحمراء” (red blood cell count). By integrating therapeutic modalities, anatomical entities, diagnostic tests, and laboratory parameters, these overlapping and complementary subsets provide a comprehensive reference for capturing procedural, diagnostic, and ancillary data, thereby supporting consistent data collection, analysis, regulatory reporting, and interoperable exchange across clinical research and patient‑care settings.
